verdict_reason: BG 2.48's tags are operator-system-coupling, incident-response, team-state. CROSS-REFERENCE WITH SAME-TAG VERSES IN AVAILABLE CORPUS: (a) incident-response also tagged on BG 2.1 ('Some verses set the scene; the teaching lives in the next sentence, not this one' — narrative scaffolding) and BG 2.2 ('Self-recrimination at viṣama-kāla is desertion in apology's clothes'), and BG 2.8 ('Naming the diagnostic limit honestly is the unlock'). 2.48 teaches: 'the operator-state should remain even across red and green dashboards; procedure invariance is the test'. 2.2 teaches: 'do not collapse into self-recrimination during the incident'. 2.8 teaches: 'name the diagnostic limit honestly'. These are COMPATIBLE under a coherent doctrine — the seasoned operator who responds to an incident with samatvam (2.48), without self-recrimination (2.2), and with honest diagnostic naming (2.8) is a single coherent character. No drift detected. (b) team-state also tagged on BG 2.5, 2.9, 2.10. 2.48's team-state stance ('over-mourning of a bad rollout breeds the blame culture') is compatible with 2.5's ('The fantasised escape is always undertaxed') and 2.9-2.10's narrative-scaffolding stance. No conflict. (c) operator-system-coupling is a NEW tag introduced by 2.48; no prior verse has it; no drift to detect. CROSS-REFERENCES: the verse does not declare cross_references[]; nothing to verify. Pass on 6.1 (compatible doctrine across same-tag verses) and 6.5 (no cross-references to dishonor). Note: 2.48 is highly compatible with 2.47 even though they share no tags — 2.47's outcome-detachment is the action-side and 2.48's operator-system-coupling is the state-side of the same doctrine; the chapter editor may want to introduce a shared tag.
